content_markdown:
The graph shows the variation of potential difference across a device with the current in the device.

Which of the following gives the resistance of the device at point X?

diagram_metadata.description: A graph of potential difference (y-axis) versus current (x-axis). The curve starts at the origin and curves upwards. Point X is marked on the curve corresponding to potential difference V and current I.

options.label: C
options.text: gradient of tangent to curve at X

options.label: D
options.text: $\frac{1}{\text{gradient of tangent to curve at X}}$

vibe_explanation: Resistance is defined by Ohm's Law as the ratio of potential difference to current ($R = V/I$) at a specific point. While the gradient of a V-I graph represents the differential resistance ($dV/dI$), the absolute resistance at any point is simply the ratio of the coordinates $V$ and $I$ at that point.
